title: amaryllis × brevifolia
intro: Amaryllis × brevifolia, also known as the dwarf amaryllis or amaryllis belladonna, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Amaryllis × brevifolia is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where its bulbs are used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Amaryllis × brevifolia is often prepared as an herbal tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, although its preparations are not widely standardized or commercially available.
